plants.name: Achacha
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are economical/commercial, orange-coloured, egg-shaped with sweet-tart flavour. Used in making fresh juice, jams, jellies & desserts. Recently commercialized in Australia.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally known in Bolivian folk medicine for digestive issues, anti-inflammatory effects, and appetite suppression. It contains hydroxycitric acid (HCA), similar to Garcinia cambogia, though at lower concentrations. Marketed in some supplements for weight management.

plants.name: Grumichama
plants.vernacularName: Grumichama Cherry / Brazilian Cherry / Spanish Cherry / Grumixama
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are eaten fresh and used in making jams, jellies, wine, and beverages. Attractive flowers and plants are used in subtropical landscaping.
plants.medicalSignificance: Potential application in nutraceuticals and functional medicine/foods. Rich in vitamin C, anthocyanins (purple pigments), polyphenols, and antioxidants. Has antimicrobial properties. Used in treating diarrhea and gastrointestinal discomfort.
